I always spoke with Brent directly not his assistant, etc. He was willing to meet with us face to face at anytime we had additional questions. He made the whole process less stressful and we were able to focus on getting better vs dealing with all the legal issues. If your looking for a good personal injury attorney look no further. You have found him:. BRENT J. SPINKS

reviews.text: I’m still getting Dr bills after my settlement and Kevin Oden tell me to call and get on a payment plan unbelievable I thought your attorney took care of all that I guess not for 40% though smh
reviews.ownerResponse: I can find no record of you hiring our firm and yet you know Kevin Oden, my employee. I'm wondering if you used another name? In any event, your medical expenses are always your responsibility to pay. Typically, if the client instructs us to pay it, we do so out of your portion of the settlement. If we issue a letter of protection promising to pay the provider, then we do so, and we negotiate that amount downward on thec client's behalf as well. I suspect in this instance you did not want your medical bills paid from the settlement and now are taking the position that we should have paid your bills for you out of our attorneys fees. No lawyer does this. Please advise if you used a different name and I'll be happy to look into it further. Brad Steele, Attorney

reviews.text: Kevin Oden was my attorney on a commercial accident. His office is a bunch of crooks. Let me start by saying my case was some what complex BUT that's why you hire attorneys right??  His assistant Lisa was always extremely nice and helpful and was able to get the cost of my medical bills down so kudos to her. When payout time came, they took their percentage off the top which was a large amount. Instead of paying all of my medical bills FIRST like an normal lawyer does, they got their cut off the highest dollar amount. Kevin was supposed to get 40% and then later told me he did me a favor and took only 30%. With this being said..he got back THOUSANDS more than me, almost 30,000 to be exact. With my payout supposed to be 40 or 30% I most deff should have still got back way more than they did. I have called several real times to address this matter and of course have not received a call back. If that's not some thiefs in the night, I don't know what is.
reviews.ownerResponse: I wanted to reply publicly to your comments of 2021. To be honest, I haven't paid a lot of attention to Google reviews but your comment, which I just recently read, really struck a nerve with me, to call us crooks. So, I went back and reviewed your file. Your mva occurred on  08-02-2020. You hired my firm 4 days later and agreed to a 40% contingency fee for our attorneys fees, which means if we don't recover any monies for you, you owe us nothing. This is a standard percentage charged by a large majority of attorneys practicing personal injury law. Our employment contract states that expenses we advance on your file are reimbursed to us first off the top of the settlement before either you or I are paid. Then, we deduct our 40% leaving you the majority, or, 60%. A lot of firms charge the expenses to the client's side but we do not. That way, the case finances itself rather than the client financing it. As you are aware, you incurred thousands of dollars in medical expenses that YOU owed for your treatment. A client's medical expense is never the responsibility of the lawyer--it always remains the client's responsibility to pay. You understood that when you hired us and when you settled your case. However, in order to put more money in your pocket, we reduced our fee from 40% to 33 1/3%. That is hardly something a crook would do. We were not required to do it but did it anyway so as to benefit you. When you say that any normal lawyer pays the clients medical off the top, you are simply incorrect. NO LAWYER DOES THAT. We prepared a written settlement breakdown and went over it with you line-by line and you signed off on it signifying that you understood it and agreed with it. We negotiated with all of your medical providers and they all agreed to reduce their bills in order to be paid. Using part of your 60%, we paid all of those bills on your behalf as we had promised the providers we would do in order for you to  receive treatment. You state that we received almost $30,000 more than you but that is not true-it's just that you owed the medical providers  for treatment that you received. Mrs. Latham, I'm sorry that you were upset about this but no one made you settle your case. You were not cheated nor were you lied to. We are not thieves in this office. In fact, I sleep well at night knowing my clients have been well-represented and treated with dignity and respect and that we have been open and honest with our clients. To the best of my knowledge, you have never called and asked to speak to me. I would be happy to discuss it with you if you do. Brad Steele, Attorney.
Thank you for the 5 star review. We appreciate it!

Allison Biggs is a distinguished criminal defense attorney based in Henderson, Texas, with over two decades of legal experience. She is renowned for her unwavering commitment to justice and her fearless approach in challenging law enforcement overreach. Clients consistently commend her for her ability to identify and address instances where authorities may have exceeded their legal boundaries.

One client shared their experience, stating, "Allison is an amazing lawyer. She took on my criminal case and resolved it swiftly and exactly what I wanted the outcome to be."

Beyond her legal acumen, Allison is celebrated for her dedication to her clients, often going above and beyond to ensure favorable outcomes. Her practice, Biggs Law Group, offers comprehensive legal services in criminal defense, family law, and personal injury.

In summary, Allison Biggs exemplifies the qualities of a formidable advocate: knowledgeable, courageous, and deeply committed to her clients' rights. Her proactive stance against misconduct and her relentless pursuit of justice make her an invaluable ally for those navigating the complexities of the legal system.
5 star service, and communication. I 100% recommend Allison!

While I ended up working my case out last minute, I spoke to Allison the week prior about my case and the complexity of it. Even though my story surrounding the case was long and detailed, she patiently listened, and asked questions along the way, in a non rushed manner, which I really appreciated. After I finished, we talked in more detail and was up front yet genuine when discussing her retainer, and I let her know I'd be getting that retainer within the week. It was semi urgent as my case was proceeding soon. I did end up getting the retainer, and updated her office staff of my progress along the way, but then reached a point where I had to make a critical decision yet I had not yet officially retained her. As other (negative) reviewers have mentioned about returning calls, I experienced the same issues, as I called the office the day before I needed to make that decision and let her staff know I had the retainer but I needed a quick recommendation asap, but unfortunately I didn't get a call back then either. HOWEVER, she told me up front, she has a smaller client base she keeps because she works hard on her cases that she's responsible for, and I believe that. I noticed other reviewers were not yet her clients either and I understand where the priorities lie. So given the situation, my urgency for her to accept a new case when she's already committed to ones she already has, I understand, does not take priority. I'm writing this positive review because of what I did see, from speaking with her, was a lot more than my impressions that I got from other firms in the area. In summary, once you retain her, I believe what the other clients in the reviews have said, that she does a great job for them. I can see that in her character. I found a way to resolve my case, after pushing my state attorney to follow through with my requests, but if that wouldn't have worked out in my favor, I would have still attempted to retain her for my case.

reviews.text: My husband and I were in a minor car accident. At first, I tried calling the insurance company of the person who ran into us by myself.   I got no where with them-not even a call back!  So, a little out of anger and,  a little out of principle, I took a chance and called Grant and Flannery.  Matt Flannery  met with us personally, and listened to our story.  His office made arrangements for us to start our treatment. Throughout the process, they made it a point to check-in on both of us.  Today, we received our settlement-and no, we are not millionaires- it was never going to be one of those exceptional cases; but that brings me to my point- knowing this was not going to be the case of year, they still treated us with the utmost respect and professionalism and never made us feel we were not important.  It was refreshing building a relationship with Matt and his staff.  I would be remiss if I didn’t mention the patience and always prepared Julia . She was an absolute delight to deal with.  Thank you to Matt, Julia and the rest of the Grant and Flannery team for making my husband and I whole again.

A bit of background - not to boast, just saying ...
I am from Canada and older, so I know quite a bit about America.  As well, I have a college degree.  So ... I thought when completing the online questions applying for citizenship, that I responded clearly (and honestly, etc.).  I took my copy to Iglesias Law just to make sure (because confusion on an application is not what you want).
David Iglesias was personable and open (only time we met) while we visited, before meeting Sonia Jurado, who was to advise me.  Although my effort was 'good enough', Sonia noticed a couple of changes that made it perfect.  As well, she provided tips and info on what to add in terms of community participation and 'really living here' proof.
My application was received by Immigration on Oct. 14; I had an in-person interview on Dec. 4!  (Your mileage may vary.)  My USCIS notice/invitation for the swearing-in ceremony is apparently in the mail already.  Whoo-hoo!
My Iglesias Law consultation was comfortable, easy, useful, and worth it, worth it.
Thanks so much for having your expertise, and helping me be less anxious about the outcome.
Mr. Iglesias handled my business in a very professional manner. I recommend him for all your legal needs.

reviews.ownerResponse: Since it has been 16 months since we last represented you, it is unfortunate that you have only recently advised us of your dissatisfaction. Clearly a lot has happened since we represented you in your legal matter.  Needless to say, we are attorneys not magicians, and cannot change the facts or the evidence of your case.  Nor can we change the choices you made that led to the results you received from a judge in litigation.  A lawyer’s duty to keep client confidences has few exceptions, and in an abundance of caution, we do not feel at liberty to respond in a point-by-point fashion in this forum, nor would you want us to.  However, we adamantly deny your allegation of prescription drug use.  What a terrible accusation to make.  Moreover, even if it were true, it speaks to your character that you would criticize someone for seeking mental health treatment.  Suffice it to say that we do not believe that your post presents a fair and accurate picture of the events.  We welcome you to contact us directly so that we may address any concerns you may have. reviews.text: I was a passenger involved in a tragic auto accident resulting in death of my driver. I had a lengthy hospital stay as well as other trauma due to the accident. I also contacted  a couple of attorney friends in Houston and College Station and asked if they knew of him. They both came back with a resounding yes he was a good selection. In working with Mr. Ace and his Para-Legal Lori P., I found them to be very professional, exhibiting honest concern, empathy, and dedication to my case. Mr. Ace kept me apprised of progress in my case every few weeks. He said there would be 2-3 phases of my case and notified me of the passage from phase 1 to 2, etc. He was very thorough and his attention to detail was exemplary. Again, I wish to say I felt he was truly concerned for my outcome and this was not just a job for him. Ms. Lori P. also demonstrated concern for my outcome as well, and she was very prompt with any request I made. Mr. Ace and Ms. Lori make an exceptional experienced team and I would highly recommend these two members of the Roberts & Roberts Law Firm. Mr. Ace went the extra mile for me and my family. Not a good attorney. Does not return phone calls or emails. Literally didn’t hear from him for a stint of 6 months. I hired him to handle a very simple case. I literally got nothing. Took my money and did not do anything he said. 3500 dollars down the drain. Do not hire this man.
If you want someone that never calls you back, never answers emails and takes forever to finish stuff … call Bruce.

I’m hoping my review helps some others understand some of the other reviews left on here. I just used him for my child custody case after I got word of mouth, that he was good. At first it was like any other consultation I’d taken. Nothing stood out or seemed special. Communication was mostly through email and some calls. I called a few times one of them he apologized and said he’d just taken on 3 other cases,  which worried me (as it would anyone). Days before my court date, I was concerned they weren’t prepare due to minimal conversation. The day before, he called asked questions and talked me through it. I felt much better and even told my family I felt he was prepared. The day of, he was very communicative. Encouraging and positive. We talked some things through before and seemed to have me relaxed. Court went much better than I even thought it would. He made light remarks to ease the court,  was extremely polite to the other family, judge, and attorney. He was stern and even stepped in when he saw I needed help. I was extremely pleased with the outcome! The judge was extremely fair to both the dad and I, considering everything. My child’s dad even told me after that Bruce was trying for more than I expected, which is what I expected and paid for. I would recommend Bruce and say the money is worth it! Important information about the case was often shared at the last minute, including being told the day before court that prison was likely after previously discussing probation. When I left an honest review about these issues, I received a response that felt more like a threat to “air dirty laundry” than a professional attempt to address concerns. That only reinforced my concerns about professionalism.

Families going through situations like this deserve clear communication, honesty, and respect. Unfortunately, that was not our experience.

reviews.text: Should be zero stars! I would not waste a penny of your money here. They make promises of what they will do but don’t follow through and keep those promises. We were withheld from watching trial all week with promises we were to testify only to be told the night before they advised against us testifying. We often showed up unnecessarily due to lack of communication on their behalf. They did nothing to fight for my family even after sinking $30,000 to them, which included hiring a PI, which didn’t happen until a week before trial. I suspect all the decent reviews never went to trial, however, we got the full trial experience and would definitely never recommend them to represent anyone I know ever again. I truly felt like this firm rolled up its sleeves and protected me against everything possible being hurled my way.  This is the only firm to call if you truly want experienced fighters on your side!!  Thank you Murphy & Baker Law Firm!!

reviews.text: Didn't even respond
reviews.ownerResponse: Hello again, James. I replied to this review 11 months ago asking for you to contact me, and I have not received a reply. We take negative reviews seriously. Unfortunately, as I said before, we have no record or recollection of any client or transaction fitting your account name. We are unable to verify anything about your identity from your name in our records. We have a record of every phone call and client interaction, and your name simply does not come up in any search. If you are a current or former client, then we want to talk to you to see if we can "fix" what went wrong. If you are not a client, however, a one-star review, without having any client relationship with our law firm, really lessens the effectiveness of Google reviews. We DO take your comments to heart, and we would like to investigate this issue further. I am once again asking for you to contact me privately. I can assure you that you will get a personal meeting or phone call with one of our attorneys. We would like a chance to repair what might have gone wrong in your experience with us to justify a one-star review. Thank you.
Thank you so much for your review. We truly appreciate it.

Not only were Geoff and his team friendly, helpful and professional but they exceeded my expectations when I came to financial compensation. After two years of working hard for my family and I, Geoff, Shannon and Becky were very patient and reassuring even when I was losing hope. Tracy is an excellent addition to the team and brought fresh energy to the Case. 10 out of 10 would definitely recommend. To Geoff, Becky, Shannon and Tracy feel free to call anytime, you'll always have a place at my table.

"Dan" is not who you are looking for. Like most attorneys, it's about money. This law firm took my case against a nursing home in September 2023. "OH, yes! We'll take your case." I only spoke to "Dan" 3 times. I gave them all the video and written evidence against this facility. I, personally,  fought FOIA to get an un-redacted report for over a year.
The "Expert" decided "He couldn't prove injury."
When asked if I had any questions, my reply should have been, "How do you sleep at night?"
So, if your loved one is ABUSED in a nursing home, don't try to get them closed down. They are state facilities and get fined over and over and then re-sold.
If your loved one doesn't survive the ABUSE, then you have a case. However,  if they do survive the ABUSE, the "Expert" will call it negligence.
What a racket. $$$$ I didn't even want money, just JUSTICE. I will leave that up to God.

As a business owner, it's not if but when, someone will try to take money from you.  I was referred to Dan Sorey and his law firm.  The best decision i made.  His expertise and care are hard to find nowadays but he exemplified both.  Thanks again and would definitely recommend them. 5 STARS

When I was lost and had no idea how to handle life moving forward I found Geoff Hoover. I cant tell you what a fantastic lawyer this man is. Him and the entire office (Becky too) did so much out of thier way to make sure we were well taken care of through the entire process. The dedication and  work that went into everything they did was amazing. He kept fighting for us. He never gave up or settled for less.  I was always kept up to date, included and discussed through every process. Losing a loved one is never easy. But, they made sure to go above and beyond so that we were taken care of. I will always appreciate him and his hard work and his how much he just cares about his clients in general.
THE BEST!! Our case lasted 3 years.  Geoff and the staff always took the time to update me whenever I had questions.  Becky is great and made us feel like we were part of the family.  THANK YOU!!!

We entrusted our case regarding medical malpractice and wrongful death to Sorey & Hoover for nearly a year, believing they would advocate for our family's interests. However, after I reached out to the firm to discuss the possibility of pursuing a claim for gross negligence—based on insights I received from a friend who had a similar experience and was successful with her attorney—we received a decline letter. It is disheartening that my inquiry about a potential legal strategy, which I thought was a reasonable discussion, might have influenced their decision to decline our case. I believe that open communication about our options should be encouraged, especially in such serious matters. My family and I are totally disappointed. My aunt who passed due to negligence at Houston transitional care skilled nursing facility will not get justice.  My family and I will have to go on and live on with this hurt in our heart that nothing was done about the death of my aunt. My family and I did seek new representation, but due to the time constraints, no one will take the case. I hope this review helps and be informative to those who are seeking honest, thoughtful, considerate representation. Be blessed

.

Best law firm hands down! Our case lasted over five years, but the entire staff was always helpful whenever I had questions. We ended up going to trial and Becky and Shannon made sure everything went smoothly for me. Since I had to travel over three hours to be in Longview, I stayed at a hotel and didn’t know the area; Everyone gave amazing recommendations for stuff while I was there. I almost ran out of clothes and Shannon was nice enough to offer her own wardrobe! (You can’t beat that!)

Dan was always a phone call or text away and made sure I was aware of what was going on throughout the process. I was lucky enough to meet Geoff the day my trial started and was very appreciative of how he guided me through the trial to keep me from being too worried. We ended up settling the case for more than we expected and I am extremely grateful to have the opportunity to end the case the way we did.

By the end of the week I was there, I was so glad to have them with me and they felt more like a family than a law firm. I would 1,000% recommend this team for any one needing a lawyer!

If you need an attorney, do yourself a favor and call Dan Sorey. You will not be sorry. Mr. Sorey, or Dan as he prefers to be called, represented our family through both a court case and the appeal. I could spend time explaining how nice he and his staff are and how great they were during the pre-trial meetings, phone calls and emails, but that is the easy part of litigation. They were, in fact, GREAT during that period, but that means nothing if they aren't prepared to face the opposing lawyers and be able to understand the nuances of your particular case and explain them to a jury. Jury trials are hard, because two sides are presenting opposing information and a jury has to believe one and not believe the other. Trust me when I tell you, you want Dan Sorey telling the jury your side. He is nothing short of amazing in-front of a jury! It is difficult to explain just brilliantly he presented our case. He rebutted not only the other side's witnesses, but the other attorney arguments with ease. He destroyed them. Completely. He had a plan, he worked the plan to perfection and was always at least 2 steps in front of everyone else in the room. If you need an attorney, call Dan. You will not be sorry. I, personally, can't thank him and his entire staff enough.

One problem everyone complains about with attorneys is communication. That has NEVER been an issue for me with Sorey & Hoover. They always respond quickly.  They are always friendly, helpful and professional.  Highly recommend!

If you ever need any legal work done I highly recommend Sorey & Hoover law firm. I was in a car accident and I found this law firm when goggling. Believe me when I say it was the best decision I could have made. If you want someone that goes to bat for you endlessly this is your firm. If ever in  need again, I surely know where I will be going.

The team at Sorey&Hoover promised to fight for my family, and they have more than delivered everything we were promised. They helped provide my rides to the doctor when I couldn't get here myself. They have treated us like Family from the start. If you've been wronged, They will make it right!!!

Dan and Geoff are two of the smartest lawyers I have met. When Dan takes on a case, he is committed to the end, and he never backs down in the fight. You want Dan and Geoff on your team! I highly recommend this firm.

I went to court this week with Dan Sorey representing me.  My main concern that morning was courthouse parking, the walk in the cold, and being early.  NOT the case.  Mr. Sorey’s background, his knowledge and preparation shown in our previous meetings, manners, and professionalism had me at ease about the case.  That was no easy feat.  Not many attorneys give clients the confidence to focus themselves on the trivial.  Dan Sorey has those great abilities.

I was very fortunate that my accountant recommended Dan Sorey.  I was very nervous about the circumstances.  I even brought him the problem encased in protective sleeves in a notebook. I’m detailed oriented.  He was not surprised.

The circumstances: someone dumped about 27,000 pounds of used materials on my property without my permission.  I removed it by law to avoid a $1,000 daily fine for every day the materials were on my property.   The person that dumped the property sued me for removing them.  I was sued for following the law!

Mr. Sorey made me feel very comfortable from  the initial meeting.  He didn’t groan seeing my notebooks.     He asked me relevant questions.  He calmed me  with facts and information immediately.  It was apparent he had prepared for our initial meeting.   My past experience with attorneys is they do no research beforehand.   Mr. Sorey told me the case was filed in the wrong court and gave me legal options.  He took care of that initial error.  The plaintiff refiled in the proper court.

It was months before the proper court date.  His staff was very professional and kept me informed.  This was very comforting in the mist of this outlandish nightmare.

His staff called that morning to tell me the time and location in the Courthouse to meet him.  He was early.  To me, it made me feel like my problem deserved his attention.  Court was late.  He kept me calm.  The plaintiff never showed up.  The case was dismissed.

I feel that the plaintiff experiencing Mr.  Sorey’s professionalism  in the original filing caused them concern.  Why they did not show up.  They wanted thousands of dollars from me.

reviews.ownerResponse: I am sorry you feel this way.  I worked very hard on your case and trial, but I cannot force a jury, after hearing the evidence, to give a ‘not guilty’ verdict.

reviews.text: He seems to be very knowledgeable on DWIs, but honestly it didn't really seem like he did a whole lot for me. While it is nice to have someone experienced in these matters to do the paperwork and correspondence with the court, beyond that there really was not a lot that he did. I thought the communication could have been better during pre court. There were things that I had brought up to him that he wanted me to look into even though this is exactly what I am paying him for... I was told I had court just a couple of days before the court date, and before this time there was no communication as to what the prosecution is offering. I was just told a couple of days before my court date what my options were and he was not real clear on the terms of the probation, and there was no talk after our initial visit of possibly taking my case to trial. It was just here is what they offered, and this is when your court date is. He comes off like he is really going to fight hard for you, but the plea deal I got is basically the standard DWI plea deal that I probably could have got with a court appointed lawyer and therefore I would have saved $6k! Also there were things on my probation that I was not even aware of until the day of my court date. Of course at this point you basically don't have much of a choice but to just go along with it and sign it. I'm not saying that he's not a good DWI lawyer, but he definitely is not worth his $6k retainer fee if all you are going to do is take a plea deal. If you're taking a plea deal you might as well just save your money and go with a court appointed lawyer. Now if you want to take your case to trial then this may be where the money would be worth it, but be prepared to pay an additional $8k if you want to take your case to trial!
reviews.ownerResponse: I can understand that it may seem like a whole lot, but so much happens 'behind the scenes' in preparation and dealing with the government. Plea deals take much more than just picking up the phone. Unfortunately, the two choices one has in dealing with the government is a plea or a trial. Either takes time and effort, but a trial takes mountains. John

reviews.text:
Edit to say-the attorney finally called me back and he “took time out of his day” to call me because I left the review. He also mentioned that he was sorry that a 1 day turn around wasn’t “good enough for me”. I proceeded to tell him, “no, that was last Thursday, not yesterday Thursday”. And then I made the point of, if I called last Thursday and said I haven’t received a call or anything, and your office did in fact send an email (it’s still unsure if I mistyped my email address or if they spelled my name wrong, my name is spelled a weird way, so it gets misspelled all the time), why wouldn’t they call me back that day or the next day and say, “I’m sorry Ms. Holt, we did send an email, did you not get it?” And then ALL OF THIS could have been avoided. But no. His response was, “Well, I can’t justify what my office staff does” or something like that. So with that, I’ll change my review to a 2 star because I finally did get a response. He stated multiple times that we haven’t hired him so there’s no relationship. I get that, but I had questions, hence why I filled out the form in the first place. Not to just get a generic email(I still haven’t received yet) saying they can’t help because the person is still employed. I wanted to talk with an attorney to get some answers.
Oh, and note at this point I’m aware that an employment attorney won’t be helpful because he is employed, it’s FMLA lawsuit we’re seeking. And his response was “I’m not going to educate you on employment law”. I’m sorry, relationship or no relationship, I feel like sometimes it is the attorneys job to educate someone who doesn’t know the law.
So all in all, I’ll update to a 2 star review, because he did call me back and because he asked MULTIPLE  times for me to change it. But it sounds like their office doesn’t have it together, he sounds like he couldn’t care less about a persons situation unless it’s going to make him money. But I could be completely wrong. I do know this, if the job is terminated, I will spend days finding another attorney, and if I can’t find one, I still won’t use Hommel.

I’d love to leave a good review, but I can’t. I called and talked to a lady over 3 weeks ago(I can’t remember if it was around 1/24 or 1/31, pretty sure it was the 24th) and explained the situation. She told me to fill out the form online so I did. Once submitted, the website said it takes 6-8 business days for the legal team to get back with me. I called back on 2/15 and left a message because I hadn’t heard back yet. Now it’s 2/22 and I still have yet to hear anything. Everyone who knows the situation says it’s a slam dunk case. I could be wrong, but the least they could do is call and say there’s no case. At that point, I’d like to know why because again…without going into details on here, it’s a solid case. 100%.
Maybe leaving a review will light a fire under the partners backside and get someone to return a phone call, then I can update my review.

Mr.Millslagle and Sarah have been there for me since the day I walked into the office. They were more worried about my mental health the case the first day and made sure I got healthy again. There’s still a ways to go but I thank GOD and Matt and Sarah for pointing me in the right direction to achieve my recovery!
They wasted no time getting my husband free and back to his family. A special shoutout to Ms.Tammy no matter how much I blew their phone up she always had an answer to my question and was very nice.

I’d like to thank the team at Files, Harrison and Millslagle. Mr. Millslagle and Mrs Carrie really pulled a blessing together and helped get my case over turned and gave me a chance to get my life back together. I’m very thankful for these two and all the hard work they done. This is a law firm that will go above and beyond and really do everything they can.

These people will be just as nice as can be until they start receiving money.  They will promise you the world and not even attempt to fulfill any of their promises.  I hired Mr. Harrison in November and I opted to make monthly payments so I could see if they were going to make good on any of their promises.  I was told since I live over 500 miles away they would do what they can to get me to be able to do pretrial drug tests where I live and would try to get the order on pretrial that I cannot be in contact with my husband lifted seeing he wasn’t pressing any charges.  None of this was even attempted on their end.  I made a call in December to make a payment and Tammy wasn’t in the office the day I called and I never heard back from her.  Finally, got a call the other day and she was wanting a payment so I tried to call to make the payment today and my credit card supposedly kept declining even though my credit company said they didn’t see any attempts to run the card on their end.  When I tried to explain to Tammy this, she kept telling me that they needed that payment, they needed that payment.  I told her I tried to pay last month and this is what I have to pay with.  I tried telling her that I keep having to drive back and forth to Tyler which is getting expensive and her response was that they were trying to get everything dropped.  She said to me on the phone, “well what’s your pretrial officers name so I can contact her so you don’t have to worry about going”.  I explained to her that my officer told me repeatedly that I have to show up even if I drug tested somewhere else to report. I also told Tammy that this was supposed to be done from the get go and I’ve already had to make a couple trips since.  Any time I tried to ask questions I was given sighs and rudely interrupted and she only had time to try to get more money out of me.  I had a good first impression but past that point was totally opposite.  It was bad enough that I had to tell her that I didn’t like her attitude and to let me know who I need to contact and that I’ll just represent myself on this matter since I’m already $1250 out.  I’m still waiting for that call back on that information, which I’m sure I’ll never receive.  Maybe that’s the name of their game, collect payment in hopes they won’t have to deal with their clients anymore.  All I can say is listen to their other bad reviews and seek someone else.  Mr. Harrison was alright from what few minutes I visited with him, but you don’t deal with him, you deal with a flustered Tammy who is his paralegal and I highly do not recommend her.  Beware!
Buck Files is Trash, he tried to get a dude to sign a deal for 35yrs. The guy got another lawyer and only ended up with 5yrs. He must work for the prosecutor

I hired Mr. Harrison for my daughter and we all are very disappointed in the lack of communication and inaction.  Whether I called, emailed, or showed up at the office, there has been no response or further contact since the initial consultation.   My daughter is sitting in jail with no contact or visit from her attorney.  We are all clueless about what is happening in the case or any plan of action.  After paying a hefty flat fee for services during this difficult time, it is very disheartening to not being able to get any information or ask any questions about the case or the legal process.  I regret not hiring an attorney that would be more involved and reachable.  We don't want all an attorney's time, just a bit of it.

I spoke to Tammy and Carrie about this situation, they got what they needed to help with my husband’s case but nothing has changed since then. My husband has been in jail for almost two years and the detainer has not been released yet. I gave Tammy the time she needed to work on this case. I did call a few times to see if she had some news for me, and she told me I needed to stop calling so much. She stated she had more important cases to work on at the moment and that she would call once she had an answer for me, plus they were in the process of moving. What all can an inmate do… other than sit and wait… right? So plenty of time, I suppose.
Two more months passed and I called to get an update, I was told to leave a message and I never heard back from them again. Why even bother calling again? They obviously don’t care.

Needless to say, I lost faith in Mr. Harrison and his team.

I contacted Mike Wallace because my father had passed and he had an active bankruptcy case at the time of his death. I tried to reach out to the attorney that originally resided over my father's bankruptcy case but he was unwilling to help or answer any questions despite me offering to pay a consultation fee for his time. I just needed some questions answered but he said he couldn't help and was quite dismissive.

So I contacted Mike Wallace to see if anything could be done so that if possible, I could prevent any of my father's creditors from coming after his estate. Mr. Wallace was very accommodating and said that if I could get him a case number, he would look up my father's case and see what could be done. Mike & Jill texted me, they called me and even set up a time that I could call back and speak with him so that they could give me an update on what he had found out regarding my father's case.

Unfortunately in my situation, there was not much that could be done because too much time had passed but I will say that I'm very grateful that Mr. Wallace was willing to help me when the original attorney my dad hired in Sulphur Springs would not. Mr. Wallace wouldn't even accept a consultation fee for his time. As I mentioned above, sometimes even when you're willing to pay people to help you, they will not always help you but Mr. Wallace took the time and effort to look into the case and give me feedback regarding this particular matter. Even though there was not much that could be done because of the length of time that passed, it really is a huge relief when you can at least get some answers to the questions that you have.

If there was something in my particular case that could have been done, I would have moved forward with Mike Wallace's services because he was willing to help me, call, text and consult with me about what he had learned regarding my particular situation. Bankruptcy is a very sensitive matter regardless if it's tied to an estate or if you're in a situation where you may have to file but if you're looking for someone who will treat you with dignity and respect and not shove you off to voicemail, I would highly recommend that you give Mike or Jill Wallace a call so that they can help you. Thank you again for your help, Teresa

reviews.text: It was shear serendipity that I found Jill and Mike Wallace when I was in a dark cave not knowing how to get out or if I would ever get out. The last choice in life I ever thought I would have to make was to file bankruptcy. To me it was an admission of failure. But when my financial burdens continued to plague me without letting up, robbing me of sleep and the ability to perform my job to my satisfaction and stealing any joy that I should have in life, I started searching for a bankruptcy attorney to get some information, already convinced that I was most likely not going to qualify for bankruptcy and that fact would throw me into further despair. I met with two attorneys and left feeling even more overwhelmed and fearful that I could not even afford to file bankruptcy, or would have to quit my job and have no income at all before I could qualify for one of the types of bankruptcy.  Thankfully, I did not give up my search hoping surely there was some attorney out there that could show me the path that would work for me. I was so impressed by the level of kindness, empathy, knowledge and professionalism that I knew immediately that God had led me to the right place. They stayed in touch with me, keep me informed, told me what to expect and how the procedure would work. Mike was at the hearing and was there to calm me down and reassure me that I would  do fine. Today, it is all behind me, thanks to Mike and Jill Wallace. I have my life back. Thank you Jill and Mike.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for telling your story here. It means more to us than we could put into words.

I was in a dire legal situation, I was taken advantage of in a nasty divorce in the worst way.  I also had false accusations brought against me in a protective order and was going to potentially be prosecuted based on lies.

I contacted Gibbins Law and hired Catherine Bateman to represent me.  She and her team sprang into immediate action and came up with a plan.  They presented undeniable truth based on fact clearly and without fault. They are experts in Law.  Thanks Catherine and Gibbins Law, I won the protective order case.
Next, Catherine and her team addressed the final decree on my previous divorce in very long and difficult hearing. The result had all previous rulings thrown out.  A new hearing with a final decree, based on fact, fairness and the law has been agreed to.   Custody of my child has rightly been awarded thanks to Catherine Bateman and her team of professionals at Gibbins Law.

Thank you so much for all you have done for my son and I.

reviews.text:
After 10 years of not feeling right about the original 5 star review I decided to be honest to myself and down grade it to 3 stars.
I wasn’t informed that I didn’t need to show up for pre trial so I showed up per court request and no Erik Platten! I then slipped out of the court room before they called my name and tried calling Mr. Platten several times with no success. After calling his legal assistant and explaining to her the situation he finally returned my call telling me he wasn’t feeling well and to just go on without him……with no representation! What a fiasco that turned out to be! There was ample evidence on the video supporting my innocence where this case should have been dismissed and never gone to the grand jury but it did and I was indicted! I insisted on a jury trial but Mr. Platten encouraged me into a plea bargain for a charge I was totally innocent!  Let me tell you it is a terrible feeling to tell a judge your guilty when your not just to make some bogus plea deal.
I do owe Eric  credit for getting me deferred adjudication “ the plea deal” and because I was a good boy I later received early termination “ one year” of a fairly  Intensive Probation sentence all for a false charge I remind you! Eric is a cool guy but cool is one thing, influential power with the local smith county DA and his cronies is another thing. And when you find yourself  spun up inside the highly corrupt smith county justice system you’re going to need all the help you can get!

Side note: This incident took place 10 years ago and I don’t know if Mr Platten is still practicing  but if he is he may have the power and know how it takes to deal with these corrupt prosecutors and judges.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your review.

After 2.5 years I finally got a resolution with the DA agreeing to drop the felony stalking charge, and I would plead to class B misdemeanor terroristic threat. It was a home run outcome for me. My career, my goals and all my future aspirations are all still intact thanks to Mr. Platten. Give him a call, you will be glad you did.

reviews.text:
Sorriest “professional” I’ve ever had displeasure of doing business with.  My husband, who had been diagnosed with Alzheimer’s, and I paid him $10,000 to plan our Estate in order to keep from growing broke.  He wanted all our assets and liabilities, which Joe and I handcartier to his legal receptionist immediately.  After a year if no comminications, despite many requests, he asked for same thing!  I send him all his nfo certified, return receipt and got notice back Myra had also signed for this.  Except this time he wanted All ORIGINALS of paperwork!  I later found out 2 things:
1.  My multi-million brother in Austin said he paid much less for a more difficult estate, and
2.originals are not required.
After a good year, I the ONLY response I got was from his paralegal, who daid each time” You haven’t gotten that yet...It’s on his desk”!
What was on his desk was Estate Summary (a’ misnomer) because thar merely recaps your assets/liabilit s...which he had...TWICE!!
An ESTATE PLAN is what he should have completed..because my husband was in a memory care home by this time.  After a final contentious phone call FROM HIM (the1st in 2-1/2 years)....I fired him.  His contract specifies NO REFUNDS, PERIOD. I spoke with Texas Bar Asdociation and they encouraged me to file two separate complaintants with two depts...one to get my money back.  By this time, I had been diagnosed with terminal lung disease, was sick, and dealing with probating my mom’s will, and other family matters...so I think time limit of 3 years expired...but I still need to verify that!  If not, Now I have time to file.  Let me add, I also went to another estate atty. who cost me $6,000 (completely screwed up Estate Plan and had ALL OUR MONEY tied up in Annunities that charged a high fee every time you took $ out!  That included our household money...thanks to his “friend” in Austin he “liked to use”.  His actions cost me $16,000 more because he didn’t save me a dime!
Long review I know, but if you ate getting Estate Planning...you HAVE to stay on top of it...I don’t care who you use ....regardless of ***** in this town.  They will lie and mislead for their benefit!
reviews.ownerResponse: I'm sorry that you feel as if you didn't get the service for which you paid, which was estate planning and medicaid assistance planning  I can only develop a plan with a client's assistance the timely receipt of documents I've requested.  For  whatever reason or reasons, that didn't happen, despite numerous requests.   I was also clear and you expressly acknowledged that you were signing a flat fee contract.  Unfortunately,  I also don't have any control over what another attorney does or doesn't do.  I wish you and Mr. Lofton well.

I normally don't leave reviews, but in this case it was warranted.  I shopped around for a few weeks and contacted several lawyers in order to obtain legal assistance with my situation.

Several of the lawyers stated it would be a very "complex" case which only served to further heighten my anxiety about getting it resolved.  Without going into too much detail, my issue concerned a property that my ex-wife and I had purchased, that was now going to be foreclosed on (even though my ex had agreed to be responsible for the payments, I was still going to suffer the consequences), not to mention my ex is someone that isn't easy to deal with in any manner.

I set up a consult and dealt with Rachael directly, we spoke about the particulars of the case, and I decided to employ the services of her and her team.

Rachael and her team were communicative, professional, and friendly from the beginning to the end of the process.  My situation, surprisingly, was handled efficiently and in a manner that was very satisfactory.  My foreclosure was halted, and I will be obtaining the property in question.

I am completely happy with the service I received from Rachael and her office, and I would recommend them to anyone that thinks they are in a tough spot.

My heartfelt thanks to Rachael and team, I truly do appreciate the work that you all accomplished on my behalf.

Best regards,
Anthony

reviews.text:
Rachael Griffith and her staff did amazing work throughout my divorce/custody case. This team is very detailed and expects the same from you so come prepared.

When I initially started working with the Griffith Law team, I was an emotional mess and struggled to gather my thoughts and evidence in a concise manner. I will tell you that operating in this state for very long will cost you financially. However, this is not a discredit to Griffith Law. Their rates directly reflect the high level of detail and time that goes into your case. It is 100% your job to gather yourself and keep your communication and demands as concise as possible. This will directly reduce how much your divorce/custody case will cost you. Unfortunately, it took me a while to learn this lesson. It was very easy to vent to my attorney, but try your best to keep good notes and send multiple issues in one communication, unless it's an emergency.

Rachael's services were just what I needed when facing a high-conflict partner who was divorcing me and trying to take my children from me. I had many accusations thrown my way for the intent of alienating me from my children. Rachael helped me fight every step of the way to keep me from losing my kids. While I didn't end up with as much visitation as I desired with my kids, I am thankful I didn't lose them completely. One thing you must understand about a high-conflict case is that you need to choose your battles wisely and always, always, always consider your children prior to taking various actions against your partner. Its easy to get caught up in how your partner hurt you emotionally, mentally, or physically. For the sake of your children's future, set that hurt aside and make them your priority as much as possible.

You will not regret using the Griffith team for your attorney needs. Again, come prepared and be ready to provide proof for every issue at hand. Keep your communication concise to reduce your overall cost for their services. Good luck.
reviews.ownerResponse: Jacob- What an amazing insight to the complexities of family law litigation. I couldn’t agree more. Thank you for the kind words as well.

Worst attorney I’ve met. Did not know my son’s name while handling my divorce/custody case. Never prepared for trial and overcharged for every phone call, email and text. And has threatened to seek legal actions for more funds following him not actually handling my case.

And I'm a disabled Veteran ... he did not care and still charged me the full consultation fee
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dennis, if I forgot your son's name in speaking with you, I apologize. I have no excuse for that. Your case didn't go to trial and was settled per your agreement. Unfortunately, many clients have buyers' remorse after settling. My job is to advocate, but if a client wants to take a deal, I become duty bound to counsel them about the pros and cons of the deal. After counseling them on the pros and cons, I cannot ignore their lawful right to settle. I work for my clients and not vice-versa. The Texas Lawyers Creed, which I'm bound to follow, states that: "I will endeavor to achieve my client’s lawful objectives in legal transactions and in litigation as quickly and economically as possible." I literally have to let clients settle if that is what they want.

As for billing, I bill for work done, nothing more, and my contract stated in great detail that I bill by the hour, like virtually all family law lawyers. Family law attorney bills can be very frustrating, because neither clients nor lawyers can predict how many billable hours will be expended. There is way too many variables. I will say your bill was in line with the average I have seen in East Texas.

As for threatening legal action: You sued me in small claims court, so I would rather not discuss legal actions between you and I.

reviews.ownerResponse:
I don't have any record of you as a client or opposing party. I've reported this as a spam/extortion review. It is common right now for random people to post reviews for businesses they know nothing about, just so they can extort business owners for money to take down the bad review. If you are reading this, I will not be blackmailed.

In any event, let me speak to your review, whomever you are. My wife and I have operated TLC Law for over 11 years in Tyler. We operate a business and we are proud to be a successful and stable law firm that supports our family and our employee's livelihood. We are only successful because of the results our client's achieve. In today's climate unsuccessful attorneys get bad reviews and don't last or grow. To become successful and help our client we charge high but market-appropriate hourly rates, so we can provide the best legal assistants and paralegals, the best resources (like legal research software, case management software, experts, IT security to keep our client's information safe, and so on). We also know that success in the family law context requires a tailored, custom approach. We don't treat any two cases alike, and this means we give each client individual attention, our passion, our energy and frankly our time. It is this time that leads to high bills, but it is also the time we spend that leads to successful results for our clients. As Abraham Lincoln said "A lawyer's time and advice are his stock in trade." So, yes, results don't come cheap whomever or whatever you are.

The bond reduction was a success and I was out of jail. During the entire time of my case they went above and beyond to fight for my innocence as well as keeping me calm and stress free. Annie and Kenneth never stopped fighting for me and standing by my side. The out come of my alleged offense went above and beyond my expectations! I’m a father and my child is 2yrs old. reviews.text:
Ms. Heitkamp represented me in a divorce and custody case that went to trial, and in the courtroom she was an absolute force. She was both a sledgehammer and a scalpel, sharp, aggressive when needed, and in control of the facts.

She is all in and expects the same from her clients. She is direct, focused, and uses every detail to build a strong case. Her team was also very responsive throughout the process.

If you need someone who is tough, strategic, and relentless in court, she is the one you want in your corner.

I’m currently in the middle of a custody process, and I’ve been incredibly impressed with this office from the very beginning. They’ve provided so much education and guidance throughout the journey, which has made a huge difference for me.

One thing I truly appreciate is the responsiveness—Ms. Heitkamp always replies to emails, no matter the time. She’s extremely thorough and makes sure she has everything needed to support you and build a strong case. She’s very blunt and expects her clients to be just as committed as she is—which I admire.

Ms. Heitkamp is a hardcore lawyer who isn’t afraid to fight. When I first met her for a consultation, I was honestly a little intimidated—but in the best way. Everyone I spoke to who knows of her said the same thing: she’s a bulldog in the courtroom and doesn’t back down. I feel confident having her in my corner.

There are no words strong enough to describe just how phenomenal Kelly Heitkamp is — not just as an attorney, but as a person. She was incredibly understanding, genuinely listened to me, and gave me advice that not only guided me legally but also gave me peace of mind during one of the most stressful times of my life. From the very first conversation, I knew I wasn’t just another case number — I was a priority.

Kelly is a powerhouse. She’s sharp, bold, and knows her job at the highest level. You can feel her experience and confidence the moment she steps in — and when she speaks, people listen. What sets her apart is her heart. She truly cares about her clients, fights with passion, and will go above and beyond without hesitation. She had my back a thousand percent, and never once let me feel alone in the process.

Her honesty is refreshing. She tells you what you need to hear, not just what you want to hear — and that kind of integrity is rare. Her staff? Just as amazing. Kind, responsive, and professional — they made me feel supported every step of the way.

In all my life, I’ve never had such an incredible experience with an attorney. Kelly Heitkamp is in a league of her own, and I’ll forever be thankful for her. She made such a powerful impact on my life that I will never forget her. She’s not just an attorney — she’s someone who becomes forever memorable to those lucky enough to have her on their side.

reviews.text: I hired Russell brown for my first appeal.After receiving my denial letter I asked to see  my file.to my horror I noted that they had not done one single piece of work. Everything in the file with the work that I had done on my initial request for my disability. His associate had apologized to me and stated that he must have dropped the ball on that one. So I began to search the market for a new attorney. However, Russell Brown told me that he had fired the associate had done such a poor job.I decided to trust him and give him another chance.it would be another 15 months before I would have my hearing with the judge. I had to call the office and let them know when the hearing was due and I had to remind them that my file needed work.and after the hearing to my horror I would be denied because of my physical status was not bad enough for disability. When all along the focus was supposed to be on my mental status not my physical status however,that has also seriously declined over the three and a half years I've been waiting to receive the disability answer. Russell Brown and associates have done a terrible job representing my case.I have now hired an attorney to help me appeal the court's decision I just pray that it's not too late. For I have been told that I may have to start this process all over again.I just don't know how I'm going to make it I'm going to lose everything. don't suffer the way I have. Find yourself a reputable attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse: I apologize that you feel that way.  Good luck to you in your future endeavors!

A Life-Changing Experience with Moran Law Firm

Working with Lisa Moran, Senior Attorney and Owner of Moran Law Firm, and her exceptional legal team was nothing short of extraordinary. Their support throughout the adoption of my younger brother was deeply personal, profoundly professional, and truly life-changing.

From our very first interaction, Ms. Moran and her team demonstrated an unwavering commitment to excellence. Their communication was impeccable—every update delivered promptly through the Clio app, emails, and personal phone calls. We were never left wondering; we were consistently informed, reassured, and cared for.

What sets Moran Law Firm apart is not just their legal expertise, but their heart. They serve families with dignity, precision, and compassion. Each team member was prepared, knowledgeable, and fully invested in our case. Their seamless coordination and unified approach gave us confidence every step of the way.

Ms. Moran herself is a rare gem—an advocate, a leader, and a source of strength. Her personal involvement and genuine care made all the difference. She didn’t just help us navigate a legal process; she helped make our family whole.

If I could give six stars, I would. Moran Law Firm is the gold standard in legal service, and I will be forever grateful for their role in one of the most meaningful chapters of our lives.

With deepest appreciation,
The Johnson Family

reviews.text:
(Dad & stepmom review)

I went through many reviews in 2022 trying to find the law firm most experienced with representing fathers. As most of us know, the system is extremely biased to most dads in the state of Texas. (Just to clarify, she represents mothers too, but I specifically hired her due to experience with dads.)

Lisa Moran takes representing her clients to a whole new level. If you want a “bulldog” in the court room, it’s her. She truly fights for what’s right & does not give up. I have never seen a lawyer fight as hard as she did throughout our entire 5 day final trial. She NEVER backed down. She truly was invested in our case doing everything she could to help us protect my husband’s daughter. I can’t say enough good things about this entire law firm. They ALL work together & each individually have so many strengths that end up benefitting each case. You won’t find a lawyer who fights harder than Lisa Moran.

I will be submitting a separate review for Sky Donovan & Lydia De La Luz who were both paralegals on our case. Not only is Lisa Moran the best, but she hires the best to work for her. These 2 paralegals honestly were the most experienced I have met! They too became extremely invested & fought hard right alongside us. They spoke up with ideas & never backed down either.

Overall, whether you’re a mother or father THIS is the law firm you need on your side. Even walking into the courtroom, you can feel the intimidation by their presence. They aren’t there to play, they’re there to fight for you. Thank you all again for everything you have done for us.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you from the bottom of my heart for this fabulous review! You and your husband became like family and reviews like this bring us more clients like you - thank you!
It was very easy fighting for your husband - he is an excellent father, and I wish all children had a dad that would fight like he did for them.

reviews.text: First of all the original lawyer, we had worked with left the firm. We called back about a year later on an enforcement issue, and the firm could not find our file. They asked us to pay a whole new orientation fee, which we did. We resent all the case details in completion. We asked them to draft a letter to the other party and were very specific on what we needed. After about 3 weeks, the letter was sent to the other party and was completely off track. They had chosen to send the letter without our review and/or approval. It was really embarrassing. We brought it to their attention, and they seemed to be offended and didn’t like our tone. They charged us for what time they had spent and returned our retainer. Bottom line, they really do not pay attention to detail. In my opinion. I know when I make a mistake I have to stand up and accept responsibility. I would look elsewhere as we have done.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. O'Dell - Although you were never a client of our firm, you did attempt to direct the handling of someone else's case.  The practice of our firm is to deal with the client in the handling of their case.

I had the pleasure of working with The Ellis Law Firm for trust preparation, and I am delighted to share my outstanding experience. Their professionalism, expertise, communication skills, and commitment to delivering timely services truly set them apart.

From the outset, the team at The Ellis Law Firm demonstrated a high level of professionalism. Their attention to detail, thorough understanding of trust preparation intricacies, and dedication to ensuring all aspects were addressed instilled confidence in their services. I felt assured that my trust was in capable hands.

The expertise displayed by The Ellis Law Firm was truly impressive. The team exhibited a deep knowledge of trust laws and regulations, providing valuable insights and guidance throughout the process. Their proficiency in navigating complex legal matters made the entire experience smooth and efficient.

Communication is a key factor in any legal engagement, and The Ellis Law Firm excelled in this regard. Clear, concise, and timely communication was a hallmark of our interactions. I was kept informed at every stage of the trust preparation process, and any inquiries or concerns were addressed promptly. This level of transparency and responsiveness greatly contributed to my overall satisfaction.

One aspect that particularly stood out was the firm's commitment to delivering timely services. Deadlines were consistently met, and the efficiency with which they handled the trust preparation demonstrated their dedication to client timelines. This reliability made the entire process stress-free and streamlined.

In conclusion, I wholeheartedly recommend The Ellis Law Firm for their exceptional trust preparation services. If you are seeking a law firm that combines professionalism, expertise, strong communication skills, and a commitment to timeliness, The Ellis Law Firm is the ideal choice. I am grateful for their exemplary service and wouldn't hesitate to engage them for future legal needs.

Initially, Mr. Hendrix offered to review and make corrections to a gift deed.  During the consultation, he emailed valuable information to me (Thank you) but reminded me that he usually charges for such information, and he also suggested that I go to the Texas Law Help site to obtain it.  If that alone were sufficient, I would not have paid him for his help.  I am just being honest so that others might benefit from this info.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Douglas - I apologize for the delay in getting to your zoom meeting; the meeting immediately prior to yours took longer than expected; I do my best not rush any of my clients. I do charge a fee for consultation meetings, and sometimes it is necessary to confirm payment before beginning the meeting. Additionally, I do charge legal fees for legal services and legal representation. I apologize for the inconvenience and delay. I recall providing you with information to assist with your legal matter, and I hope that it was useful.
Let me know if I can be of assistance in the future.

I cannot say enough good things about this law office. I’ve had the privilege of working with Sarina during my divorce and Lily for a custody battle. Both were absolutely incredible. They are not only sharp and knowledgeable attorneys, but also compassionate and truly invested in their clients.

The entire team of paralegals is phenomenal. Everyone is kind, patient, and always willing to help or answer questions, no matter how small. Their support during such emotionally exhausting chapters of my life meant more than they will ever know.

What really sets this office apart is that they genuinely care. You are not just another case or number to them. They care about you as a person, your family, and the outcome of your case. It is not just about the money, and that means everything when you’re going through something hard.

I refer everyone I know to this office. One of my close friends is now a client because of my recommendation, and I will continue sending people their way.

Highly, highly recommend. They are truly the best. Thank you all for everything you’ve done for me.

My family and I will be forever grateful to Attorney Matt Bingham and his exceptional right hand assistant, Arin Vilo, at the Law Offices of D. Matt Bingham, PLLC. Facing an unfair and challenging situation that had weighed on me for months, I walked into their office feeling anxious and uncertain. From the very first moment, Arin Vilo greeted me with kindness and genuine attentiveness, making me feel heard and supported. When I met Attorney Matt Bingham, his honesty and straightforward approach gave me immediate confidence. He took the time to carefully explain every step of the process, ensuring that I understood my options and what to expect moving forward. After signing my contract, I felt a renewed sense of hope, knowing that my family, my peace, and my career were in the hands of a team who truly cared.

What could have been a long and distressing ordeal estimated at 12 months to a years was resolved in less than a month thanks to Matt Bingham and Arin Vilo to their dedication and expertise. I will never forget the call when he personally delivered the incredible news that my case had been dismissed. In that moment, I felt an overwhelming sense of relief, and my life quickly returned to normal. Attorney Matt Bingham commitment not only saved me but restored my family’s peace of mind.

There are many attorneys in Texas, but few possess the integrity, compassion, and skill that Matt Bingham brings to every case. Many may claim to be the best, but it is the quality of care and attention that truly set Attorney Bingham and his team apart. If you are searching for someone who will listen, fight for you, and deliver real results, look no further than the Law Offices of D. Matt Bingham, PLLC. They deserve to be recognized as the number one choice for legal representation, and I recommend them wholeheartedly to anyone seeking justice.

Being an ex District Attorney for the city of Tyler area for many years,  Matt Bigham  is very knowledgeable about the law and how the system works and  has dealt with many high and low  profile cases and knows the in's and out's  in all degrees.   Attorney Matt Bingham goes  out of his way to  explain things on the level where you can understand.  He  and his assistant Arin are both very polite and helpful and  treats you like a  person instead of just a number coming through.  Matt or his  assistant Arin, always returned my phone calls the same day without fail.
He got my adult son off probation earlier with serving only half the time he was initially given. This was something Matt Bingham did while my son was  actually serving the initial probation term he was given.  Matt Bigham did this service extra without charging the extra $1,200   fee  he normally charges for this service. Matt Bingham  is a man of high INTEGRITY.  He tells you he is going to do something and does  exactly what he says.    I HIGHLY recommend  Matt Bigham as an attorney. You will feel comfortable knowing you have an attorney that's smart, professional, will do right by you and is one of the top attorneys out there working on your behalf. I  wrote this sincere review because i thought it would be HELPFUL  for someone that was searching for a great attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for such a kind and thoughtful review Cheneta! We enjoyed getting to know you and your son and are so glad we were able to help him get this behind him.

reviews.text: Words can’t express the immense level of gratitude that I have for Matt and his team (though I’ll try). This past August, I was stopped while driving on the road back home to my family. Without getting into the weeds of my situation, I’ll say that one traffic stop seemed as though it would change the trajectory of my life. While searching for legal representation, my family and I spoke with almost ten different attorneys based in Smith County. I can EASILY say my conversations with Matt were the most comforting and restorative to my confidence in my pending freedom. From the very first call, Matt was insightful, knowledgeable, and assuring. Not only that, but he and Arin were also prompt in responding to my questions before I even selected him to represent me. Now that I am in the next chapter of this whole ordeal, I am so thankful that I had Matt in my corner to help see things through. And while I’d never hope that you/your loved ones would need legal representation; if you do, I would HIGHLY recommend that you speak to Matt!

reviews.text:
This is really important to me; writing this review. I mean everything I say here.
What I was accused of was, in my opinion, the worst possible crime on earth. The things I was charged with carried a possibility of 2 life sentences. Those were the circumstances under which I met Matt and Arin for the first time and I swear to you my innocence was never in question to either of them and I felt extremely comfortable going into that office and explaining something I was absolutely terrified of anyone else even knowing; much less talking about. My situation was beyond stigmatizing, but Matt‘s team did such an amazing job of explaining to me all the different ways we could get to the truth and get a just outcome in my case and that alone was priceless.

My situation was particularly taxing because the District Attorney in my case was particularly unresponsive. For that reason, things would drag on for 15 days short of 2 years. During that time, for 2 years straight, Matt and Arin went above and beyond anything I ever expected. They poured every resource available in my case into eliminating one hurdle after another. Right off the bat Matt was able to get a judge to reverse their decision to place me on house arrest and order me to wear an ankle monitor at all times. Next, after speaking with that same judge, I was allowed to literally move out of state 1,200 miles away so that I could continue working in my field and without that by itself, being able to work, I have no idea where I’d be right now. For me to be allowed to do that is unheard of, but because of what Matt was able to pull off I was able to live as normal of a life as possible the last 2 years while awaiting a decision from the DA to either dismiss or go to trial.

The time Matt and Arin dedicated to me and my case was simply out of this world. There were times Matt and I were in the phone at 9, 10, or 11 o’clock while you’d expect any other lawyer in the world to leave their work phone on the desk when they go home at 5. Matt and Arin both made time to just talk and listen to me vent on a personal level about how that case was effecting my life and the empathy and concern and compassion they showed me meant more than I can write in a simple review, but I can at least say that these people deeply care about you and fighting on your behalf actually matters to them. That’s why they were in constant communication with me over the years through email, through text, in person and over the phone. They never missed a call, no email or text ever went unanswered, and every question I had was usually answered before I could even ask because they know exactly what they’re doing.

Being Matt’s client put me in touch with several people who didn’t work for Matt and were not affiliated with the law firm in any way, and every single time, every person I ever spoke to, they all told me the same things. They said the best decision I ever made in my life was hiring Matt Bingham because he’s the best there is. People who just know Matt by reputation will all tell you he’s the best lawyer in the entire state and the fact that I’m here and able to write this review is proof that his work speaks for itself. Matt has a name and his name carries so much weight and respect that ultimately the people who would be prosecuting me and seeking a life sentence decided they would rather dismiss my case entirely without ever going to pre trial rather than walk into a court room in front of a judge and jury and go to war with Matt over the future of my life. Think of the worst possible crime you can be charged with, then imagine a lawyer so good the prosecution would rather drop all charges than take you to trial. That’s what I got when I was fortunate enough to hire Matt as an attorney.

Listen, I could talk for weeks about what Matt and Arin went though with me, but the most important thing is this: if you’re charged with a crime they already have you in their sights, you’re already one wrong move away from losing everything. Hiring Matt is your last chance to get your life back. Call him.
reviews.ownerResponse: It was an honor to represent you, get to know you, fight for your innocence, and finally get these cases dismissed. We are so glad you got your life back. We really appreciate you taking the time to type up such a meaningful review.

Mr. Eric Dana Jensen, You want to be sure you remember his name. Because, this so called “lawyer”  displays very, very poor etiquette. I was calling to inquire, about possibly retaining his services. He was very hyped up for some reason, agitated, divisive, and offensive, as if, I was being intrusive of his time. He was so rude, and obtuse, he kept interrupting me and acted as if I owed him money or something (e.g. his clients on payment plans). I am an intellect and a professional, that lives an extremely comfortable life. I am not bragging, it’s just when he mentioned his fee, he implied as if I couldn’t afford him, I assured him I easily could. He hung up on me, then when I called back to inquire why, he wanted to put me on his calendar to make an appointment (hell no). The attorney that recommended him, was my attorney prior, up to a couple of weeks ago. He abandoned my case, stating he had developed depression, and told this, to the people I hired him to protect me from. When I asked Mr. Jensen, if they were friends he became very abrasive and hung up on me again. I noticed that Mr. Jensen, was threatening to sue a former client for defamation.  I do not believe he has much financial means in life, if he wants to sue someone he took money from. I have proof about everything I stated on here, so Mr. Jensen if you are derogatory towards me or even make a comment about anything I have put on here. I know exactly what I need to do next in this situation. I really want people to know, to please find someone who will protect you and your rights. Please do not learn the hard way, as I did.

reviews.text: I was a passenger involved in a tragic auto accident resulting in death of my driver. I had a lengthy hospital stay as well as other trauma due to the accident. I also contacted  a couple of attorney friends in Houston and College Station and asked if they knew of him. They both came back with a resounding yes he was a good selection. In working with Mr. Ace and his Para-Legal Lori P., I found them to be very professional, exhibiting honest concern, empathy, and dedication to my case. Mr. Ace kept me apprised of progress in my case every few weeks. He said there would be 2-3 phases of my case and notified me of the passage from phase 1 to 2, etc. He was very thorough and his attention to detail was exemplary. Again, I wish to say I felt he was truly concerned for my outcome and this was not just a job for him. Ms. Lori P. also demonstrated concern for my outcome as well, and she was very prompt with any request I made. Mr. Ace and Ms. Lori make an exceptional experienced team and I would highly recommend these two members of the Roberts & Roberts Law Firm. Mr. Ace went the extra mile for me and my family.
